01_initial_request.target_name: High Potential Iron Sulfur Protein (HiPIP) PioC
01_initial_request.input_data: PioC protein sample, 13C detected NMR experiments measuring longitudinal relaxation rates (R1) of 13C' and 13Cα nuclei, existing 1H PRE and classical NMR restraints
01_initial_request.user_intent: To validate 13C based Paramagnetic Relaxation Enhancement (PRE) restraints as a complementary, non-redundant source of structural information to 1H PREs for refining the solution structure of paramagnetic metalloproteins near the metal center
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Measurement of 13C R1 relaxation rates at short distances from the paramagnetic center converted into distance restraints to improve the quality and quantity of NMR information for structure refinement

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PioC protein from Rhodopseudomonas palustris TIE-1 contains a [Fe4S4]3+/2+ cluster and is stable in the reduced [Fe4S4]2+ form. [Gap] Uniformly labeled protein sample needed for 13C detected NMR experiments. [Decision] To achieve structural refinement goal, chose to use M9 minimal media expression system to perform protein expression and purification on PioC, expecting to obtain uniformly 15N, 13C labelled PioC protein sample.

parameters.media_components: M9 minimal media with ammonium sulfate (15N2, 99%) and [U-13C6] D-glucose

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Successfully expressed and purified uniformly 15N, 13C labelled PioC protein

references.citation: Trindade, I.B. et al., 2020, Biomol. NMR Assign. 14, 211-215
references.relevance: Previously reported detailed procedure for PioC expression and purification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 13C' longitudinal relaxation rates can be collected via HNCO-type experiment. [Gap] Need to measure R1 values for carbonyl carbons using 1H detection. [Decision] To achieve relaxation rate measurement goal, chose to use Bruker Avance III 700MHz spectrometer with TXI probe to perform HNCO-T1 experiments on PioC, expecting to obtain 13C' R1 relaxation rates.

parameters.probe: 5mm cryogenically cooled inverse detection probe head (TXI)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: HNCO-T1 experiment was hardly susceptible to experimental optimization for fast relaxing signals; many R1 values could not be properly measured due to efficient paramagnetic relaxation during HNCO block

references.citation: Zeng, L. et al., 1996, J. Biomol. NMR 7, 157-162
references.relevance: HNCO-type experiment for 13CO longitudinal relaxation measurement

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 1H detected experiments have limitations for fast relaxing signals in paramagnetic systems. [Gap] Need more robust method for 13C' R1 measurements near paramagnetic center. [Decision] To achieve reliable relaxation rate measurement goal, chose to use Bruker AVANCE NEO spectrometer with CT-TXO probe to perform IR-COCA-AP experiments on PioC, expecting to obtain 13C' R1 values for fast relaxing spins.

parameters.probe: 5mm cryogenically cooled probe head optimized for 13C direct detection (CT-TXO)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: IR-COCA-AP experiment was the most reliable experiment for C' R1 measurements, especially for R1 rates larger than 4-5 s^-1; obtained signals also contained in HNCO-T1 and CON-T1

references.citation: Bertini, I. et al., 2005, J. Magn. Reson. 174, 125-132
references.relevance: 13C direct detected experiments optimization for paramagnetic signals

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proline preceding residues are missing in HNCO-T1 experiments. [Gap] Need to measure R1 values for carbonyl carbons of Proline preceding residues. [Decision] To achieve complete relaxation rate measurement goal, chose to use CON-T1 experiment to perform R1 measurements on Proline preceding residues of PioC, expecting to obtain 13C' R1 values for these residues.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Successfully obtained R1 values for Proline preceding residues which were missing in HNCO-T1

references.relevance: CON experiment implementation for 13C R1 measurements

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 13Cα nuclei longitudinal relaxation rates need to be measured for complete structural information. [Gap] Need to measure R1 values for slow relaxing 13Cα nuclei. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive relaxation rate measurement goal, chose to use IR-CACO-IPAP pulse sequence to perform R1 measurements on slow relaxing 13Cα nuclei of PioC, expecting to obtain 13Cα R1 values.

parameters.spectrometer: 700MHz NMR spectrometer with 13C direct detection probe

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Successfully measured R1 values for slow relaxing 13Cα nuclei

references.citation: Pontoriero, L. et al., 2020, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 59, 18537-18545

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fast relaxing 13Cα signals require optimized experiment. [Gap] Need to measure R1 values for fast relaxing 13Cα nuclei. [Decision] To achieve complete 13Cα relaxation rate measurement goal, chose to use IR-CACO-AP experiment to perform R1 measurements on fast relaxing 13Cα nuclei of PioC, expecting to obtain 13Cα R1 values for fast relaxing signals.

parameters.spectrometer: 700MHz NMR spectrometer with 13C direct detection probe
parameters.decoupling: waltz65 and garp4 for 1H and 15N decoupling

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Combined approach provided R1 Cα values in the range 1.5-20 s^-1

references.citation: Bertini, I. et al., 2005, J. Magn. Reson. 174, 125-132
references.relevance: CACO-AP most efficient experiment in presence of strong paramagnetic relaxation effects

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw NMR spectra need to be processed for analysis. [Gap] Need to process all spectra with appropriate weighting functions. [Decision] To achieve data processing goal, chose to use Topspin software to perform spectral processing on all NMR spectra, expecting to obtain processed spectra ready for analysis.

parameters.input_data: all NMR spectra from relaxation experiments

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All spectra processed successfully with squared cosine weighting functions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Processed spectra need peak intensity integration for relaxation analysis. [Gap] Need to integrate cross-peak intensities from inversion recovery experiments. [Decision] To achieve peak quantification goal, chose to use CARA software to perform cross-peak intensity integration on processed spectra, expecting to obtain integrated peak intensities for relaxation analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Peak intensities integrated for all signals using Computed Aided Resonance Assignment software

references.citation: Keller, R., 2004, The Computer Aided Resonance Assignment Tutorial
references.relevance: CARA software for NMR peak integration

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Integrated peak intensities need to be fitted to extract relaxation rates. [Gap] Need to analyze relaxation data and fit exponential decays. [Decision] To achieve relaxation rate extraction goal, chose to use Origin software to perform three-parameter exponential decay fitting on relaxation data, expecting to obtain R1 relaxation rates for all nuclei.

parameters.input_data: all relaxation data from peak integration

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All relaxation data analyzed; R1 values obtained and summarized in Table 2

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] R1 relaxation rates need to be converted into distance restraints for structure calculation. [Gap] Need to factorize paramagnetic contributions and convert to upper limit distances. [Decision] To achieve distance restraint generation goal, chose to use paramagnetic relaxation enhancement equations to perform R1 to distance conversion on measured relaxation rates, expecting to obtain 13C PRE-based distance restraints.

parameters.R_dia_estimation: average of R_obs values of residues 5-7 (far from paramagnetic center)
parameters.Cys_tolerance_adjustment: Cα increased by 15%, C' increased by 5%

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained 27 13C' and Cα PRE-based distance restraints; R1 Cα values in range 1.5-20 s^-1

references.citation: Bertini, I. et al., 2017, NMR of Paramagnetic Molecules
references.relevance: PRE theory and distance conversion equations

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] New 13C PRE restraints need to be incorporated into structure calculation. [Gap] Need to refine PioC NMR structure with extended restraint set. [Decision] To achieve structure refinement goal, chose to use CYANA software to perform structure calculation with 13C PRE restraints added to existing restraints, expecting to obtain refined NMR structure of PioC.

parameters.restraint_types: NOEs, backbone dihedral angles, 1H PREs, 13C PREs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Structure obtained with extended restraint set; backbone RMSD between mean structures 0.43Å; two structures identical within uncertainty

references.relevance: CYANA for automated NMR structure calculation

references.citation: Trindade, I.B. et al., 2021, FEBS J. 288, 3010-3023
references.relevance: Previously calculated and deposited PioC structure (PDB: 6XYV)

03_success_verification.validation_technique: NMR structure comparison and restraint consistency analysis

backbone_RMSD_between_mean_structures.interpretation: Backbone RMSD between mean structures with and without 13C PREs indicates the two structures are identical within uncertainty

backbone_RMSD_family_with_13C_PREs.interpretation: Backbone RMSD of 0.43±0.10Å observed for family with 13C PREs

backbone_RMSD_family_without_13C_PREs.interpretation: Backbone RMSD of 0.46±0.11Å observed for family without 13C PREs

distance_dispersion_reduction.interpretation: Addition of 13C restraints results in lower dispersion of calculated distances, particularly for residues in 5-9Å sphere from cluster

restraint_compatibility.interpretation: Small increase in target function indicates new set of restraints is fully compatible with previous one

total_13C_PRE_restraints.interpretation: 27 C' and Cα constraints added to structure restraints

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was successfully achieved. 13C based PRE restraints were validated as a complementary, non-redundant source of structural information to 1H PREs. The 13C R1 relaxation rates were successfully measured at short distances from the paramagnetic center and converted into distance restraints. The addition of 13C PRE restraints improved the quality and quantity of NMR information for structure refinement, with reduced distance dispersion particularly in the 5-9Å region from the cluster, while maintaining structural consistency with the previous structure (backbone RMSD 0.43Å between mean structures).
